summary: High-quality implementation that fully satisfies all 12 acceptance criteria. Code is well-tested (19 tests passing), properly error-handled, and maintains backward compatibility. All issues identified are minor quality improvements that do not affect functionality.

minor.problem: Uses dynamic import() for existsSync instead of static import, adding unnecessary overhead on every role load.
minor.suggestion: Use static import at top of file: import { existsSync } from 'node:fs';

minor.problem: detectTechnology parameter typed as 'any' instead of proper Sandbox type, weakening type safety.
minor.suggestion: Import and use proper type: import type { Sandbox } from '@ai-hero/sandcastle'; then use Sandbox type for parameter.

minor.problem: Error handler in detectTechnology silently swallows all errors without logging, making debugging harder.
minor.suggestion: Add debug logging: log.detail(`Technology detection failed: ${error}. Falling back to generic roles.`);

minor.problem: Nested try-catch in loadRoleWithTech silently catches file read errors without logging.
minor.suggestion: Add debug logging: log.detail(`Failed to read ${techSpecificFile}: ${error}. Falling back to generic role.`);

minor.problem: Detection command searches entire directory tree including node_modules/, .git/, etc., which could be slower on large repos.
minor.suggestion: Consider excluding common non-source directories: find . -name '*.csproj' -type f -not -path '*/node_modules/*' -not -path '*/.git/*' 2>/dev/null | head -n 1

minor.problem: Technology-specific guidance files could include more concrete code examples to demonstrate patterns.
minor.suggestion: Consider adding brief code snippets in future iterations (e.g., Assert.Throws example for C#, expect().toThrow() for TypeScript).

minor.problem: Detection logic hardcoded for only C# and TypeScript, requiring code changes to add new technologies.
minor.suggestion: Consider refactoring to configuration-based approach in future for better extensibility.
